raw_consumer_statement: on XXXX I tried to charge a repair for {$1800.00} to my citi bank card. It was declined twice for some unknown reason. So I used cash and another card. I assumed it was because I hadn't used that card in quite a while. So I find out the next day it was charged on the citi bank card without authorization or my signature. I called and told the vendor to expect them to take back the money. He was fine with it and didn't understand why or how they completed the transaction in the first place. I disputed the charge and explained the issue. They wanted me to show proof that I paid another way. I sent them copies of my cash receipt and my wells fargo card receipt for the balance. I also sent the owners name and phone number for them to contact him. I also asked for a signed receipt from the transaction and they told me they didn't provide that type of thing. They denied my request and charged it to my account. I again raised the dispute and sent more info via certified mail. They denied it a second time and have since denied me a third time. I have advised my attorney about this and he suggested I file with you and to keep everything on paper and not via phone calls. I've had this account for 25 years and have a credit rating over XXXX. I have since closed the account. I've never had a credit card company treat me like this.
